data.description:

data.title: Biomerieux drops as it warns of larger FX impact on 2025 CEBIT
data.description:
** Shares in Biomerieux drop more than 5% after the French in-vitro diagnostics firm said currency exchange (FX) would have larger impact on 2025 core earnings than previously guided

** It confirms 2025 guidance for sales and contributive operating income before non-recurring items (CEBIT)

** However, it expects FX to have a negative impact of around 35-40 million euros ($39.8-45.5 million) on 2025 CEBIT vs around 30 million before

** It posts Q1 sales of 1.10 billion euros, up 12.7% organically

** Shares on track for worst day since September 2022 if the losses hold

** They are among top fallers on France's SBF 120 index

($1 = 0.8796 euros)

data.title: Alistithmar AREIC Diversified REIT Fund Declares Dividend for Unitholders
data.description:
Alistithmar AREIC Diversified REIT Fund (SASE:4350) declared a cash dividend of 0.375 Saudi riyal per share for the period starting from Sept. 4, 2024, to Dec. 31, 2024.

The dividend will be paid within 15 days to Saudi real estate investment traded fund's unitholders of record on April 30, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: India's Infosys falls ahead of fourth-quarter results
data.description:
** Infosys, India's no. 2 IT firm , falls 1.2% to 1,396 rupees ahead of its Q4 results

** Analysts, on avg, expect INFY to report 1.9% sequential growth in Q4 rev, and close to 9% Y/Y rise - as per data compiled by LSEG

** Q4 profit seen falling 0.5% Q/Q, but expect 8.65 Y/Y climb - LSEG

** Most of the large IT companies will register revenue decline ranging from -1.8% to +0.1% Q/Q (-1.6% to +6% in YoY terms) due to a weak demand environment, lower billing days, and lower discretionary spends - HDFC Securities

** Rivals Wipro and TCS both flagged tariff-related uncertainty resulting in delays in clients' decision-making

** WIPR among 6 stocks on 10-member IT index rated "hold", TCS and INFY rated "buy"- LSEG

** WIPR down 26% YTD vs IT index's 25% decline

data.title: Awfis Space Solutions Signs Pact to Launch Corporate Transportation Services
data.description:
Awfis Space Solutions has partnered with ECOS (India) Mobility and Hospitality to launch corporate transportation services, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company will offer a suite of transport services, including corporate car rental, employee shuttle, and carpooling solutions, premium chauffeured services for executives and VIP clients, the flexible workspace solutions provider said.

It will also offer hotel travel desk services, including airport transfers, and self-drive options.

Shares of the company were up over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: FireFly Metals Limited: Deferred Consideration Payment and Cleansing Notice
data.description:
Green Bay Copper-Gold Project Acquisition

Baie Verte, Newfoundland--(Newsfile Corp. - April 17, 2025) - FireFly Metals Limited ("FireFly" or the "Company") is pleased to advise that it has now paid in full the aggregate consideration for the acquisition of Rambler Metals and Mining Canada Limited (now FireFly Metals Canada Limited) and 1948565 Ontario Inc. (collectively "Rambler Group") and, through the Rambler Group, a 100% interest in the Green Bay Copper-Gold Project located on the Baie Verte peninsula of north-east Newfoundland, Canada ("Acquisition") (see ASX announcements dated 31 August 2023 and 20 October 2023).

As announced in its ASX announcement on 20 October 2023, the Company has previously completed the Acquisition. A portion of the purchase price for the Acquisition, comprising A$7.5M in cash and A$7.5M in cash or shares (at the Company's election) ("Deferred Consideration") was agreed to be paid within 18 months following completion. The Company has now paid the Deferred Consideration.

As part payment of the Deferred Consideration, the Company issued 9,778,357 fully paid ordinary shares ("Shares").

The Appendix 2A confirms that the issue of the Shares takes up part of the Company's placement capacity under ASX Listing Rule 7.1.

FireFly issued the Shares without disclosure under section 708A(5) of the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) ("Act"). With reference to the Shares issued, in accordance with section 708A(6) of the Act, the Company gives notice under paragraph 708A(5)(e) that:

1. the Shares were issued without disclosure under Part 6D.2 of the Act; and

2. as at the date of this notice:

a. the Company has complied with the provisions of Chapter 2M of the Act as they apply to the Company;

b. the Company has complied with sections 674 and 674A of the Act; and

c. there is no excluded information within the meaning of sections 708A(7) and 708A(8) of the Act which is required to be disclosed under section 708A(6)(e) of the Act.

This announcement has been authorised by the Board of Directors.

Steve Parsons

Managing Director

FireFly Metals Ltd

Phone: +61 8 9220 9030

Media

Paul Armstrong

Read Corporate

+61 8 9388 1474

Not for distribution to United States newswire services or for dissemination in the United States.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/248896

data.title: Benzene prices journey southward in Asia
data.description: Benzene prices trended downward in Asia on Wednesday.An industry source in Asia informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices were assessed lower on the back of a bearish purchase pulse in the Asian markets."On Wednesday, FOB Korea benzene prices were assessed at the USD 720-730/mt levels, a drop of USD (-15/mt) from Tuesday's assessed levels.CFR China benzene prices on Wednesday were assessed at the USD 730-740/mt levels, a day on day decline of USD (-20/mt).Meanwhile, downstream SM prices on Wednesday were assessed at the USD 905-915/mt FOB Korea levels, down USD (-15/mt) from Tuesday.

data.title: Workspace Group Rental Income, Pricing Rise
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Workspace Group said both rent owed to it and pricing rose in its fourth quarter, though it expects recent macroeconomic events and slower economic growth to continue to hit customer sentiment.

The office-rental company warned Thursday that the impact of larger tenants leaving, a trend it expects to continue in the first half of fiscal 2026, will take time to overcome but said it remains focused on things it can control. This includes costs as it continues to prioritize a recovery in occupancy and rent roll, an industry metric reflecting rental revenue owed by tenants.

Workspace said that for the period ended March 31, rent roll increased by 0.8% to 139.4 million pounds ($184.6 million). On a like-for-like basis, rent roll grew 1.4% in the quarter to 107.9 million pounds.

Lettings activity was strong, with 390 new lettings completed and total rental value rising by 15% on quarter to 10.1 million pounds, a 15% rise from the fourth quarter of the year before.

Like-for-like rent per square foot grew 0.9% on quarter to 48.08 pounds, while like-for-like occupancy fell by 1 percentage point to 85.1% in the period.

Looking ahead, Workspace said it expects customer demand to be quieter in the first quarter due to the timing of bank holidays.

London-listed shares were down 0.2% in early trade at 425.50 pence.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: UK's Dunelm rises to 3-month high on robust results
data.description:
** Shares of British homeware retailer Dunelm up 6.23% at 1,011p, highest since January 2025

** Top gainer on London's mid-cap index

** Co sees biggest intraday gain since July 2024

** DNLM posts 6.3% YoY growth in Q3 FY25 sales to 462 million pounds ($611.60 million)

** Co expects to meet FY25 PBT guidance of between 204 million pounds to 214 million pounds

** Peel Hunt analysts say, "Management is alive to any opportunities that may open up due to tariff disruption, notably any window to secure lower factory gate pricing or other advantage"

** Up to Wednesday's close, stock fell 7.06% so far this year

($1 = 0.7554 pounds)

data.title: REG-Correction: AS Tallinna Vesi will hold an Investor Conference Webinar to introduce the results for the 1st quarter of 2025
data.description:
AS Tallinna Vesi invites its shareholders, investors, analysts, and other stakeholders to join its investor conference webinar, introducing the results for the 1st quarter and 3 months of 2025.

The webinar is scheduled for 25 April 2025 at 10:00 am (EET) and will be held in English. The webinar will be hosted by Aleksandr Timofejev, Chief Executive Officer, and Taavi Gröön, Chief Financial Officer.

The questions will be answered by Aleksandr Timofejev and Taavi Gröön after the presentation. We encourage participants to send their questions before the webinar takes place, by 9:00 am (EET) on 25 April at the latest, to kommunikatsioon@tvesi.ee. Questions can also be asked during the webinar.

To join the webinar, please register via following link.

The registration will be open until 25 April at 9:00 am (EET). You will receive a link to the webinar.

The webinar will be recorded and made available online for everyone on the company’s website at www.tallinnavesi.ee and on AS Tallinna Vesi YouTube account.

Taavi Gröön

Chief Financial Officer

AS Tallinna Vesi

(372) 62 62 200

kommunikatsioon@tvesi.ee
